items.title: Kelantan Regent receives Fahmi, discusses fake social media accounts and current issues
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, June 17 — The Regent of Kelantan, Tengku Muhammad Fakhry Petra, consented to receive the Minister of Communications, Datuk Fahmi Fadzil at the Kota Lama Palace. The Kelantan Sultan’s Media Office in a statement today informed that the audience session was intended to brief the Regent on the latest developments and reports related to the scope of the Ministry of Communications’ work. Accompanying Fahmi at the event was the Senior Private Secretary to the Minister of Communications, MohamadAsif Afifi Mohd Yusof, the Minister of Communications accompanying officer, Tuan Ahmad Afifi Hamdan Tuan Aziz, and staff from the Minister of Communications’ office.

items.title: Gold recoups losses as oil prices fall on Iran deal
items.summary: FUNDAMENTALS Spot gold rose 1.5 per cent to US$4,322.41 per ounce by 0102 GMT after declining 1.7 per cent on Wednesday. US gold futures for August delivery fell 0.9 per cent to US$4,343.10. Spot silver rose 2.2 per cent to US$69.51 per ounce, platinum gained 1.8 per cent to US$1,767.53, and palladium was up two per cent at US$1,338.67.

items.title: Johor BN set to unveil candidate line-up on Saturday, says Onn Hafiz
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Johor BN is expected to finalise its candidate list for the state election by the end of this week, with an announcement likely to be made on Saturday. Johor BN chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi said the candidate selection process for the 16th Johor state election is 80 per cent complete, although the final line-up remained subject to changes pending approval from the coalition's top leadership. Onn Hafiz, who is also the incumbent Machap assemblyman, said BN's candidate selection criteria centred on service, acceptability and local familiarity rather than age.

items.title: Dollar clings to two-month peak as Fed rate-hike bets mount
items.summary: HONG KONG: The US dollar clung to a more than two-month high on Thursday as markets ramped up wagers on Federal Reserve rate hikes this year, heaping fresh pressure on the Japanese yen towards intervention territory. The US central bank held rates steady in a 3.50 per cent-3.75 per cent range as new chair Kevin Warsh opened a new era with a sweeping policy review. The risk-sensitive Australian and New Zealand dollars were both up about 0.2 per cent at US$0.7025 and US$0.5780, respectively.

items.title: Labuan SAR operation for eight-year-old boy feared drowned in Sungai Ganggarak Permai continues into night
items.summary: LABUAN, June 18 — A search and rescue (SAR) operation is underway for an eight-year-old boy feared drowned in Sungai Ganggarak Permai here yesterday evening. Labuan Fire and Rescue Department director Abdul Rahman Ali said the department received an emergency call on the incident at 6.36pm. Abdul Rahman said 14 personnel from the Layangan Fire and Rescue Station were deployed, assisted by members of the Tanjung Aru Water Rescue Team (PBS) and police personnel.

items.title: Statutory body CEO to face seven graft charges over RM1mil sports facility bribes
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A chief executive officer of a statutory body will face seven corruption charges at the Sessions Court today for allegedly soliciting and accepting bribes amounting to more than RM1 million. The suspect, in his 40s, is expected to be charged in connection with a multi-million-ringgit sports facility upgrading project. Preliminary investigations revealed that he had allegedly demanded RM1 million as an inducement to approve payment claims related to the project.

items.title: #NSTViral: 'Waterfall view' in property listing near TTDI raises concerns [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A property sales pitch near Taman Tun Dr Ismail (TTDI) has drawn concern after a real estate agent advertised a unit with a "waterfall view" that appeared to be heavy rainwater runoff from a slope. The post, shared on Threads by user @hanahdbee, included a video showing water cascading down the slope during a downpour. His living hall can see a waterfall," the user wrote.

items.title: UPSI denies involvement in viral ‘mualaf registration’ poster
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 18 — Universiti Pendidikan Sultan Idris (UPSI) has denied being involved in a poster about registering “saudara baharu” or “mualaf” that went viral on social media recently. The university said through its social media channels that the poster dated June 15 was never brought up to the university’s administration for consideration or approval, and that it was spread without using any official UPSI channels. The public is advised to always refer to the university’s official announcements through its official mediums,” the university said.

items.title: Malaysia eyes stronger trade, energy and investment ties during Anwar’s Turkmenistan visit
items.summary: ASHGABAT (Turkmenistan) June 18 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim’s visit to Turkmenistan from June 18 to 19 is highly significant as it is expected not only to create more opportunities for cooperation in the oil and gas sector but also to enhance collaboration in other industries such as transportation, logistics and agriculture between the two countries. Malaysia’s Ambassador to Turkmenistan, Mohd Suhaimi Ahmad Tajuddin, said the official visit is also expected to provide both sides with opportunities to explore greater cooperation in trade and investment, higher education and tourism, while opening avenues for Malaysian companies to expand exports, particularly in the halal and palm oil industries. Mohd Suhaimi said the visit, undertaken at the invitation of Turkmenistan President Serdar Berdimuhamedov, also reciprocates the president’s official visit to Malaysia on December 18-19, 2024.

items.title: Google Gemini co-lead Noam Shazeer to join IPO-bound OpenAI
items.summary: BENGALURU: Noam Shazeer, a vice president of engineering at Google and co-lead of its Gemini artificial intelligence models, said on Wednesday that he will leave the company to join IPO-bound OpenAI, the maker of ChatGPT. Shazeer's move comes as leading AI firms compete for talent while racing to develop advanced models. The timing of Shazeer's departure from Google was not immediately clear.

items.title: Deputy communications minister backs former Federal Court judge Nallini for Malaysia Media Council chair, cites press freedom rulings
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 18 — Deputy Communications Minister Teo Nie Ching has expressed confidence in former Federal Court judge Tan Sri Nallini Pathmanathan’s ability to lead the Malaysian Media Council (MMC), citing her distinguished judicial record, including notable dissenting judgments on press freedom and constitutional rights. Teo noted that the Malaysian Media Council was finally established in 2025 after five decades of advocacy, petitions and policy dialogues. Nallini was appointed chairperson of the MMC, the self-regulatory body established under the Malaysian Media Council Act 2025.

items.title: South Korea to apply zero tariffs on LNG and LPG
items.summary: SEJONG: South Korea will apply zero tariff rates on liquefied natural gas (LNG) and liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) imports within quota limits in the second half of 2026 as it seeks to ease inflationary pressures amid continued volatility in global energy prices. The Ministry of Economy and Finance said tariffs on LNG, LPG and crude oil used in LPG production will be reduced to zero from July 1, replacing an earlier plan to lower LNG tariffs to two per cent in the third quarter and one per cent in the fourth quarter, while cutting LPG-related tariffs to one per cent in the second half. The government said the move is expected to help stabilise consumer prices by lowering utility and transportation costs, as inflation rose 3.1 per cent year-on-year in May, the fastest pace in 26 months.

items.title: Bersatu's fate in PN down to supreme council majority vote
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bersatu's future in Perikatan Nasional (PN) will be decided by a majority vote in the PN supreme council meeting. PN Youth chief Afnan Hamimi Taib Azamudden said if the supreme council fails to decide on Bersatu's status, the matter will be referred to the Presidential Council for a consensus. He also said that Bersatu was prepared to face Pas in the Johor and Negri Sembilan state elections if negotiations to repair ties between the two parties failed.

items.title: Malaysia deports two wanted Indian fugitives back to India over Punjab railway blast
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 18 — Two wanted Indian fugitives believed to be involved in a railway track explosion in Patiala, Punjab, on April 27, have been deported to India by the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM). Bukit Aman Criminal Investigation Department (CID) director Datuk M. Kumar said the deportation followed a request from Indian authorities to locate and detain the two individuals. He said the operation highlights the close ties and cooperation between Malaysia and India in tackling serious crimes involving regional security.

items.title: Johor polls: Teo Nie Ching warns of fake DAP posters aimed at spooking voters
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 18 — The public is urged not to be influenced by smear tactics and false information, including the manipulation of posters involving potential DAP candidates for the Johor state election. Johor DAP chairman Teo Nie Ching said certain parties had deliberately altered the posters, portraying the potential candidates as Muslim women with improperly worn headscarves. She said the act was intended to instil fear among non-Malay voters, particularly the Chinese community, and to discourage them from voting for Pakatan Harapan (PH) in the Johor state election.

items.title: HSBC Australia faces US$24.6mil penalty over scam protection failures
items.summary: BENGALURU: HSBC's Australian unit has admitted to serious failures in protecting customers from scams and could face a A$35 million (US$24.59 million) penalty, subject to court approval, Australia's corporate regulator said on Thursday. The Australian Securities and Investments Commission (ASIC) and HSBC will jointly seek approval from the Federal Court for the proposed penalty. ASIC said the case is among the first globally to target a bank's handling of scam risks and customer protection failures.

items.title: ‘Wait for official statement’: PAS says no decision yet on Perikatan developments
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 18 — PAS has not reached any decision on the latest developments in Perikatan Nasional (PN), its information chief said today. Ahmad Fadhli Shaari said various issues, including current developments within PN, were discussed during the party meeting, but no announcement would be made at this stage, Harian Metro reported. “In this democratic country, all parties can contest,” he said.

items.title: Dentist, disabled entrepreneur put purpose beyond profit
items.summary: IPOH: For Dr Farah Shazwani Mohamd Tarmizi, her work as a dentist and non-governmental organisation leader is part of a single purpose shaped since childhood. That sense of service, she said, began at the age of 9 when she would accompany her father, Mohamad Tarmizi Abdul Hamid, to religious talks and community programmes. Dr Farah said the Siti Khadijah Award (Women Personality) she received at the state Maal Hijrah celebration is the result of the team supporting her work.

items.title: Oil slips again as US, Iran sign peace deal
items.summary: BEIJING: Oil prices fell in early trading on Thursday after the US and Iran signed an interim agreement to end the war, reopen the Strait of Hormuz and waive US sanctions on Tehran's oil exports, resolving the largest energy supply disruption in history. Brent crude futures fell 89 cents, or 1.12 per cent, to US$78.66 a barrel as of 0005 GMT, while US West Texas Intermediate crude declined 98 cents, or 1.28 per cent, to US$75.81 a barrel. The benchmarks resumed their decline, reversing gains made on Wednesday after US President Donald Trump said he could resume his bombing campaign if Iran's leaders "don't behave".

items.title: Hamzah Zainudin attends PAS-hosted Opposition MPs’ meeting in Kuala Lumpur
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 18 — Former Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia deputy president, Larut MP and founder of the Reset movement Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin arrived at PAS headquarters here on Wednesday for a meeting involving Opposition lawmakers. Also present were Beluran MP Datuk Seri Ronald Kiandee, Indera Mahkota MP Datuk Seri Saifuddin Abdullah and Masjid Tanah MP Datuk Mas Ermieyati Samsudin, reported Sinar Harian . Earlier, Pasir Mas MP Ahmad Fadhli Shaari said the meeting was expected to cover a range of current political developments, including issues surrounding Perikatan Nasional.

items.title: Japan's Nikkei gauge crosses 71,000 on US-Iran peace deal
items.summary: The broader Topix gained 1.67 per cent to 4,080.26. Chip-related shares led the Nikkei's gains, with Socionext jumping 9.19 per cent and Screen Holdings rising 4.96 per cent. Financial stocks also advanced, with Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group up 2.44 per cent.

items.title: Malaysia-Turkmenistan ties set for boost as Anwar visits Ashgabat
items.summary: ASHGABAT (Turkmenistan): Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim's June 18 to 19 visit to Turkmenistan is expected to boost bilateral cooperation, particularly in oil and gas, as well as sectors such as transportation, logistics and agriculture. Malaysia's Ambassador to Turkmenistan, Mohd Suhaimi Ahmad Tajuddin, said the visit would also pave the way for greater collaboration in trade and investment, higher education and tourism, while creating opportunities for Malaysian companies to expand exports, especially in halal products and palm oil. Suhaimi said the visit, undertaken at the invitation of Turkmenistan President Serdar Berdimuhamedov, also reciprocates the president's official visit to Malaysia on Dec 18-19, 2024.

items.title: Anwar: International engagement key to strengthening understanding between nations
items.summary: KAZAN, June 18 — In a world that continues to face various challenges and uncertainties, platforms for engagement, such as the Asean-Russia Commemorative Summit and bilateral relations between countries, are highly meaningful for strengthening mutual understanding, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. He said such summits are also important for building trust and exploring areas of cooperation that can deliver mutual benefits to Asean member states and Russia. Speaking after attending a gala dinner hosted by President Vladimir Putin at the Asean-Russia Commemorative Summit, Anwar said the event provided an opportunity to foster closer ties among participating leaders.

items.title: Iran reveals 14-point draft outlining US deal roadmap
items.summary: According to Anadolu Ajansi, the document outlines a framework to end hostilities, launch negotiations toward a final agreement, and address sanctions, maritime security, Iran's nuclear programme and reconstruction. KEY PROVISIONS INCLUDE: 1. Negotiations on a final agreement within 60 days, extendable by consent.

items.title: Anwar praises Putin’s consistent stance on Gaza and Iran
items.summary: KAZAN, June 18 — Malaysian Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has expressed appreciation to Russian President Vladimir Putin for the country’s consistent position on Gaza and the current geopolitical situation involving Iran. During their meeting here today, Anwar said Russia had maintained a principled stance on both issues. Anwar said Malaysians and many others admired Putin’s steadfastness and conviction in upholding those positions.

items.title: Putrajaya in disrepair: Broken lifts, escalators, and cracked tiles mar city's appeal [WATCH]
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Public squares and facilities here are showing signs of disrepair, with residents and traders raising concerns over maintenance. Tourists visit there every day," commented user Ahmad. Well-maintained public facilities reflect our country's image."

items.title: Anwar, Putin discuss energy security and expanding trade in local currencies
items.summary: KAZAN, June 18 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim and Russian President Vladimir Putin discussed various aspects of bilateral cooperation, including the potential to strengthen ties in the energy sector, one of the strategic areas for both countries. In a statement issued after the meeting, Malaysia’s Prime Minister’s Office said Anwar and Putin also discussed efforts to expand economic cooperation, including Malaysia’s appreciation of Russia’s commitment to long-term cooperation in energy and oil supplies. “They also exchanged views on opportunities to strengthen cooperation in new technologies, cybersecurity, digitalisation, artificial intelligence (AI), modern agriculture and pharmaceuticals,” according to the statement.

items.title: Anwar thanks Putin for backing Malaysia-Russia energy cooperation, Petronas
items.summary: KAZAN, June 18 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim expressed appreciation to Russian President Vladimir Putin for Russia’s support in strengthening energy cooperation with Malaysia, especially Petroliam Nasional Bhd (Petronas). Anwar said bilateral mechanisms between Malaysia and Russia had expanded across various fields, including trade, investment, the halal economy and finance. Russia is aiming to abolish visa requirements for citizens of Malaysia, Indonesia and Kuwait this year.

items.title: Ringgit opens easier against US dollar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR:The ringgit opened lower against the US dollar on Thursday as sentiment turned cautious after the US Federal Reserve adopted a hawkish tone, driving US Treasury yields higher. Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said the US Dollar Index (DXY) rose 0.85 per cent to 100.382 points after the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) signalled a more hawkish monetary policy outlook. "Two-year US Treasury yields rose 13 basis points to 4.18 per cent.

items.title: Putin: Russia, Malaysia well placed to deepen ties as 60th anniversary nears
items.summary: KAZAN, June 18 — Russian President Vladimir Putin said Russia and Malaysia have established a strong foundation for further expanding bilateral cooperation as both countries approach the 60th anniversary of diplomatic relations next year. Speaking during a meeting with Malaysian Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim on the sidelines of the Asean-Russia Commemorative Summit here on Wednesday night, Putin said the two countries were approaching the milestone with “substantive positive experience” in bilateral engagement. He welcomed Malaysia’s support for strengthening the strategic partnership between Russia and Asean, noting that the first summit-level meeting between Russia and Asean was held in Malaysia in 2005.

items.title: Perikatan shake-up: Radzi, Azmin removed as coalition revamps leadership ahead of Johor, N. Sembilan polls
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 18 — Perikatan Nasional (PN) today announced a restructuring of its leadership, with the services of Bersatu vice-president Datuk Dr Mohd Radzi Md Jidin and Bersatu secretary-general Datuk Seri Mohamed Azmin Ali terminated with immediate effect. PN chairman Datuk Seri Ir Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar said the move was part of preparations for the upcoming Johor and Negeri Sembilan state elections. In a statement last night, he said Mohd Radzi, who previously served as PN election director, had been replaced by Kedah Menteri Besar and PAS election director Datuk Seri Muhammad Sanusi Md Nor.

items.title: Anwar, Putin discuss energy security, trade in local currencies
items.summary: KAZAN: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim and Russian President Vladimir Putin discussed strengthening bilateral cooperation, focusing on energy security and expanding trade. In a statement, the Prime Minister's Office said Anwar and Putin exchanged views on long‑term energy collaboration and oil supplies, alongside opportunities in new technologies, cybersecurity, digitalisation, artificial intelligence (AI), modern agriculture and pharmaceuticals. "The Madani government will continue to take strategic and proactive measures to strengthen energy security, including through international cooperation that ensures stability, continuity and resilience of supplies for national prosperity," the statement said.

items.title: Jalan Semabok-Ayer Keroh shut due to sinkhole
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A 20m stretch of Jalan Semabok-Ayer Keroh here has been closed to traffic after a burst water pipe formed a sinkhole yesterday. The Melaka Tengah Public Works Department said the incident occurred at 9am near the traffic light at Masjid Bukit Bayan, along Section 3.4 of Federal Route 264. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: I have no choice, says mum who delivers food with special child in tow
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Strapping her daughter to her chest, Azimah Abd Rahim weaves through traffic delivering food. She has tried leaving her daughter with babysitters, but every attempt ended in mistreatment. Azimah turned to food delivery work during the Covid-19 Movement Control Order after delays in welfare assistance.

items.title: Historic breakthrough as UM reaches 56th in QS rankings
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Universiti Malaya (UM) has achieved its highest‑ever position in the QS World University Rankings, rising from 58th to 56th. Among Malaysian universities, UM ranked first in Academic Reputation, Employer Reputation, Employment Outcomes and Sustainability. UM also secured strong positions in International Research Network (125th) and International Students (153rd).

items.title: Russia, Malaysia well placed to deepen ties, says Putin
items.summary: KAZAN: Russian President Vladimir Putin said Russia and Malaysia have built a strong foundation for expanding cooperation as both countries approach the 60th anniversary of diplomatic relations next year. Speaking during a meeting with Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim on the sidelines of the Asean‑Russia Commemorative Summit, Putin said the milestone comes with "substantive positive experience" in bilateral engagement. Putin welcomed Malaysia's support for strengthening the Russia‑Asean strategic partnership, recalling that the first summit‑level meeting was held in Malaysia in 2005.

items.title: 'Overweight' call kept on consumer sector
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The consumer products sector is expected to remain defensive amid market volatility, underpinned by resilient domestic consumption and earnings visibility, according to RHB Research. In a note, the firm said fiscal support from the Sumbangan Asas Rahmah (Sara) programme, alongside fuel subsidies, should help sustain household spending and contain inflationary pressures. RHB Research has maintained an "overweight" stance on the sector, with top picks, including Nestle (Malaysia) Bhd, Farm Fresh Bhd, MR DIY Group (M) Bhd, and Eco-Shop Marketing Bhd.

items.title: Anwar commends Putin's stance on Gaza and Iran
items.summary: KAZAN: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim expressed appreciation to Russian President Vladimir Putin for Russia's consistent position on Gaza and the geopolitical situation involving Iran. During their meeting today, Anwar said Russia had maintained a principled stance on both issues. Geopolitically, Gaza and Iran remain key Middle East issues attracting the attention of major powers, including the United States, Russia and China.

items.title: Free-float ringgit regime seen as effective amid volatility, say economists
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's free-floating ringgit regime, supported by Bank Negara Malaysia's reliance on non-interest rate interventions, is viewed by economists as effective in managing currency volatility. The central bank's policy framework, together with liquidity management measures, has helped anchor the ringgit around the RM4.00 level this year, despite periods of volatility. Economist Dr Geoffrey Williams said the latest fluctuations largely reflect external factors, particularly widening US–Malaysia interest rate differentials.

items.title: Economists see room for Brent crude to ease toward US$70 as supply stability supports downside risk
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Brent crude may have further downside risk, with economists saying prices could ease toward pre-conflict levels of around US$70 per barrel if global supply routes remain stable and no new disruptions occur. At the time of writing, Brent crude futures fell to US$83.68 per barrel, while US West Texas Intermediate (WTI) stood at US$80.75 per barrel. Najib said that for Malaysia, a move in Brent crude towards US$70 per barrel would be disinflationary.

items.title: Farm Fresh targets Cambodia dairy market with 3Q plant launch
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Farm Fresh Bhd is accelerating its regional expansion with a new dairy processing plant in Cambodia expected to commence full operations in the third quarter of this year, positioning the company to capitalise on growing demand for locally supplied milk products in a market historically dominated by imports. Once operational, it will house four production lines with an annual capacity of 20 million litres of pasteurised milk and 40 million litres of ultra-high temperature (UHT) milk, said Farm Fresh group chief financial officer, Mohd Khairul Mat Hassan. Farm Fresh began laying the groundwork for its Cambodia expansion in mid-2025, following geopolitical tensions between Cambodia and Thailand that disrupted established supply chains and created opportunities for new market entrants.

items.title: Anwar thanks Putin for backing energy cooperation with Petronas
items.summary: KAZAN: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim expressed appreciation to Russian President Vladimir Putin for Russia's support in strengthening energy cooperation with Malaysia, particularly Petroliam Nasional Bhd (Petronas). Anwar said bilateral mechanisms between Malaysia and Russia have expanded across trade, investment, the halal economy and finance. Anwar thanked Putin for Russia's support, noting that Petronas had engaged with Rosneft and other companies to advance collaboration.

items.title: 'Overweight' call kept on consumer sector
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The consumer products sector is expected to remain defensive amid market volatility, underpinned by resilient domestic consumption and earnings visibility, according to RHB Research. In a note, the firm said fiscal support from the Sumbangan Asas Rahmah (Sara) programme, alongside fuel subsidies, should help sustain household spending and contain inflationary pressures. RHB Research has maintained an "overweight" stance on the sector, with top picks including Nestle (Malaysia) Bhd, Farm Fresh Bhd, MR DIY Group (M) Bhd, and Eco-Shop Marketing Bhd.

items.title: Why subsidies sound costly but incentives sound strategic
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: As global oil prices climb amid sustained tensions in the Middle East, Malaysia's fuel subsidy debate has returned with renewed urgency. But when governments support corporations, the language changes. We speak of tax incentives, investment promotion, strategic grants, industrial policy and competitiveness packages.

items.title: Missiles rain down on Kyiv as explosions rock Ukrainian capital
items.summary: KYIV: Kyiv came under attack from missiles in the early hours of Thursday, according to AFP journalists in the Ukrainian capital. The head of Kyiv's military administration, Tymur Tkachenko, confirmed that the city was under missile attack, but that there were no immediate reports of casualties or damage. US President Donald Trump, once reluctant to support Kyiv, said on Tuesday during the G7 summit in France that Moscow should "make a deal" and indicated Washington could reimpose waived sanctions on Russia.--AFP © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: RTMKlik plays defence as VPN users try to sneak in World Cup access
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 17 — Radio Televisyen Malaysia (RTM) has set up a special team and system to monitor attempts to access its FIFA World Cup 2026 broadcasts from outside the country via the RTMKlik platform, including through Virtual Private Networks (VPNs). Director-General of Broadcasting Ashwad Ismail said that continuous monitoring is carried out with strategic technological partners to ensure that tournament broadcasts can only be viewed within Malaysia, in line with the broadcasting rights agreement with the International Federation of Association Football (FIFA). Ashwad said the measure also aims to ensure the smooth broadcast of all 104 matches through RTM and its official broadcast partner, Unifi TV, while maintaining the stability of the digital platform throughout the tournament.

items.title: PM Anwar discusses Asean-Russia ties, culture and global issues with international media in Kazan
items.summary: KAZAN, June 17 — From Asean-Russia cooperation and energy security to Russian literature and the humanitarian crisis in Gaza, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim fielded a wide range of questions from international media on the sidelines of the Asean-Russia Business Forum here. Anwar said closer collaboration could be pursued in areas including energy security, cybersecurity, agriculture, digital technology, scientific research and higher education. The Prime Minister also described his recent meeting with Russian President Vladimir Putin as productive, particularly in discussions involving economic cooperation and energy security.

items.title: Diesel price in peninsular Malaysia now RM4.37 a litre, cheaper by 30 sen; RON95 and RON97 prices maintained
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 17 — The retail price of diesel in Peninsular Malaysia has been reduced by 30 sen to RM4.37 per litre for the week of June 18–24, following a decline in international market prices, the Finance Ministry said in a statement today. The ministry said the adjustment was made under the Automatic Pricing Mechanism (APM) and reflected average movement of international market prices in the previous week, noting that easing tensions in West Asia had helped moderate global crude oil prices compared to recent peaks. For the June 18–24 period, retail prices are as follows: RON97: RM4.35 per litre (unchanged) RON95: RM3.72 per litre (unchanged) Diesel (Peninsular Malaysia): RM4.37 per litre (down 30 sen) Subsidised fuel prices remain unchanged, including RM1.99 per litre for RON95 under BUDI95, RM2.15 per litre for diesel in Sabah, Sarawak and Labuan, and RM2.05–RM2.15 per litre under the SKPS and SKDS schemes.

items.title: PM Anwar: Asean-Russia should expand cooperation more broadly, especially in energy and food security
items.summary: KAZAN, June 17 — Asean and Russia need to expand multilateral cooperation in areas such as food security, energy, advanced manufacturing, digital technologies, and education, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. He noted that these sectors offer significant opportunities for collaboration as Asean member countries and Russia seek to diversify markets, strengthen supply chain resilience and explore new avenues for growth. “Despite the uncertain global environment, Asean has demonstrated remarkable resilience.

items.title: Puad Zarkashi ends six‑term run in Rengit, says age makes re‑election inappropriate
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 17 — Johor legislative assembly speaker Datuk Mohd Puad Zarkashi has confirmed that he would not defend his incumbent Rengit seat in the upcoming state election. “In fact, I conveyed my intention to Umno president Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi and the party’s Johor state chief Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi about two weeks ago,” he said in a posting on his official Facebook page today. Puad, who is also an Umno supreme council member, said Onn Hafiz, as the Johor Menteri Besar, should be given the opportunity to lead the younger generation and the state government.

items.title: ‘Not chicken feed’: 1MDB judge says Najib’s reaction to US$681m deposits not that of a reasonable man
items.summary: In his 809-page judgment released yesterday, 1MDB trial judge Datuk Collin Lawrence Sequerah said evidence such as bank records and the money trail showed that the US$681 million was actually misappropriated from 1Malaysia Development Berhad (1MDB), also rejecting Najib’s claim that it was an Arab donation. The judge pointed out that Najib did not verify if the money he received was actually donations from Saudi Arabia. But the judge said Najib didn’t verify if the money was actually from King Abdullah.

items.title: Perak Perhilitan relocates herd of 12 wild elephants from Kampung Bongor to forest reserve
items.summary: GERIK, June 17 — The Perak Department of Wildlife and National Parks (Perhilitan) has managed to relocate a herd of 12 wild elephants in the area of Kampung Bongor in a recent operation. Its director Mohd Taufik Abdul Rahman said that the elephants were moved in stages from the village since Monday with the help of two female elephants Indah and Suria and will be relocated to their natural habitat far from human settlements. “All adult and young elephants found were estimated to be about one and a half years to 35 years old,” he said when contacted by Bernama, adding that the department received complaints from villagers about the presence of elephants in their area recently.

items.title: Maintenance worker, 24, drowns during water tower tank repair in Bandar Saujana Perdana
items.summary: Selangor Fire and Rescue Department operations centre chief Hamdan Hamzah said the 24-year-old victim was found unconscious on top of the tower reservoir tank before being pronounced dead at the scene. “The fire engine arrived at the scene at 12.17pm and found the victim unconscious. Hamdan said firefighters subsequently retrieved and brought the victim down from the tank.

items.title: Frameworks alone not enough, Anwar urges businesses and innovators to drive Asean‑Russia cooperation
items.summary: KAZAN, June 17 — Finalisation of the Asean-Russia Strategic Programme on Trade and Investment Cooperation 2026-2035 is a shot in the arm for the next leap in economic ties, but an enabling environment is key to turning them into reality, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today. The programme, set to be adopted later this year, provides the roadmap for the next phase of economic relations between the 11-member bloc and Russia, focusing on trade and investment, emerging technologies, and climate resilience. “Economic resilience should complement international cooperation, not replace it,” he said.

items.title: Nanta confirms he will remain politically active, laughs off speculation on next move
items.summary: KUCHING, June 17 — Gabungan Parti Sarawak (GPS) secretary-general Datuk Seri Alexander Nanta Linggi remained tight-lipped on Wednesday over whether he would defend his parliamentary seat or transition to state politics. Pressed about his political future amid ongoing speculation over candidate negotiations for the coming Sarawak election, Nanta said the matter had already received extensive media coverage. Nanta, who is also Parti Pesaka Bumiputera Bersatu (PBB) secretary-general and Kapit MP, indicated that there were plans for his next political move but said he was not ready to disclose further details.

items.title: Kelantan police say petrol smuggling at Malaysia‑Thailand border remains low, controlled despite West Asia tensions
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, June 17 — Petrol smuggling activities along the Malaysia-Thailand border, particularly in Kelantan, remain at a low and controlled level despite concerns over the impact of ongoing tensions in West Asia on regional fuel supply. According to him, the situation remains manageable due to proactive and coordinated enforcement measures conducted through large-scale integrated operations at border entry points and petrol stations within identified risk zones. Mohd Yusoff said daily control operations and patrols conducted by the General Operations Force (GOF), together with police personnel from nearby stations, would continue to be intensified at identified petrol stations to prevent any leakage of subsidised fuel.

items.title: Hobart Camp grenade blast kills soldier’s daughter Siti Khadijah, who dreamed of following father’s legacy
items.summary: KLUANG, June 17 — A retired soldier’s dream of seeing his youngest daughter continuing the family’s military legacy ended in tragedy when Private Siti Khadijah Sungip, 24, was killed during a grenade exercise at Hobart Camp in Gurun, Kedah, yesterday. The victim’s elder sister, Siti Zaiha, 39, said Siti Khadijah sent a photograph via WhatsApp at midnight on June 15, which is now the family’s last keepsake. “Siti Khadijah said she had more training the next day and that the grenade exercise would start around 8am.

items.title: Minister warns false claims of 60,000 backdoor entries risk eroding trust in higher education
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 17 — The allegation that between 30,000 and 60,000 students gained admission to public universities (UA) through the ‘backdoor’ is a serious accusation that could undermine public confidence in the integrity of the country’s higher education system. Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abd Kadir said the baseless claim not only tarnishes the reputation of public universities but also creates the perception that opportunities meant for qualified students had been taken away for financial gain. He said such a narrative could erode public trust, particularly among parents and students currently undergoing the admission process to higher education institutions (IPTs).

items.title: Mass fish kill in Danau Kota: DBKL explains why heavy rain may be to blame
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 17 — City Hall (DBKL) has responded to reports of a mass fish kill at Taman Danau Kota Lake yesterday, confirming that comprehensive clean-up operations were completed on the same day. “DBKL, alongside the Federal Territory’s Department of Environment, also conducted water sampling on June 16 for quality analysis to pinpoint the exact factors contributing to the incident,” DBKL said. DBKL noted that Taman Danau Kota Lake serves a dual purpose: it is a recreational park that also functions as a flood retention pond.

items.title: Malaysian Army chief orders immediate halt to grenade drills after deadly accident in Kedah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 17 — The Malaysian Army has halted all grenade technique exercises with immediate effect following a deadly training incident at Hobart Camp, Kedah, which killed two soldiers yesterday. Army chief General Tan Sri Azhan Md Othman said the suspension is a necessary step to facilitate a comprehensive probe into the incident, NST reported. “We have suspended grenade-throwing training with immediate effect to allow investigations to be carried out,” he said.
